title: draba lasiocarpa subsp. dolichostyla
intro: Draba lasiocarpa subsp. dolichostyla, also known as the alpine whitlowgrass, is a plant species within the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of North America, where it grows in rocky outcrops and alpine meadows. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Draba lasiocarpa subsp. dolichostyla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
